Guys you are not overriding the right package - the only modification I made was to `camera_android` and you need to override that just as I specified in my comment. Overriding the camera package from my fork will change nothing because I didn't modify anything there.

@acoutts I do not know how to do that? If I have both camera and camera_android in my pubspec, I get this:

Because camera 0.10.0+1 depends on camera_android ^0.10.0 and no versions of camera match >0.10.0+1 <0.11.0, camera ^0.10.0+1 requires camera_android from hosted.
So, because frontier depends on both camera ^0.10.0+1 and camera_android from git, version solving failed.
pub get failed (1; So, because frontier depends on both camera ^0.10.0+1 and camera_android from git, version solving failed.)

> Guys you are not overriding the right package - the only modification I made was to `camera_android` and you need to override that just as I specified in my comment. Overriding the camera package from my fork will change nothing because I didn't modify anything there.

This worked for me! On Pixel 6a with Android 13. Thank you!

@acoutts's solution fixes the issue but it causes another error on other version Pixel phones. So I forkd it and changed his tempory fix and make it conditional for only Android 13, so limiting possible side effects. I sent pull request, after his approval it should apply to everyone who already uses his git package as dependency_overrides. But for now anyone use it from my git package if necessary:

```yaml
dependency_overrides:
  camera_android:
    git:
      url: https://github.com/elmar001/plugins.git
      path: packages/camera/camera_android
      ref: 5172b44088a4082ff0899594614c5380457d341b
```

After some research, it seems like the problem is in the Android SDK 33 code itself.

Prior to SDK 33, the flow `CamcorderProfile.getAll(cameraId, quality) -> native_get_camcorder_profiles(id, quality)` was working properly and returning the videoprofiles.

Since SDK 33, the flow now adds some logic to return advanced encoders `CamcorderProfile.getAll(cameraId, quality) -> native_get_camcorder_profiles(id, quality, advanced)` and returns `[null]` as videoprofiles just as was previously mentioned here.

I think that there is nothing to be done in Flutter side unfortunately.

I do not think Android developers will change anything. I had one Flutter issue reported thru issue tracker almost 3 month now, they did not do anything to clarify or solve the issue. Maybe we should implement some other way in Flutter side.  I'll have a look how Android 13 native app behaves in this situation.

> I do not think Android developers will change anything. I had one Flutter issue reported thru issue tracker almost 3 month now, they did not do anything to clarify or solve the issue. Maybe we should implement some other way in Flutter side. I'll have a look how Android 13 native app behaves in this situation.

I tried it in a Native Android application. The result is just the same. 

As I said, it is an issue with the `android.media` library and its methods. More precisely, with the JNI methods called inside.

Well, you are just launching the camera. That will of course work. You need to test the methods from the `android.media` library that actually fail in api 33. Specifically:

```java
//.java 
var recordingProfile = CamcorderProfile.getAll("0", CamcorderProfile.QUALITY_LOW); //returns [null] in videoProfiles

List<EncoderProfiles.VideoProfile> videoProfiles = recordingProfile.getVideoProfiles(); //Crash because null
```

@stuartmorgan As per https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/109769#issuecomment-1286690214, this issue looks like an issue with some Android APIs. If this is the case, does this make this issue invalid?

Does this mean that until the Android APIs get fixes (if ever), we can't use this plugin with `targetSdkVersion 33`? Does this mean that no Flutter app that uses the camera can properly support Android 13?

> @stuartmorgan As per [#109769 (comment)](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/109769#issuecomment-1286690214), this issue looks like an issue with some Android APIs. If this is the case, does this make this issue invalid?

Not necessarily; even if the issue is with specific Android APIs, we may be able to work around it.

Hello there! 
I found that forcing a `false` value to the `advanced` argument of the native implementation of `CamcorderProfile.getAll` is fixing the crash. At least for me. Probably original value of `CompatChanges.isChangeEnabled(RETURN_ADVANCED_VIDEO_PROFILES)` may be wrong, or something like that.

I did it like this:
https://github.com/posxposy/plugins/blob/main/packages/camera/camera_android/android/src/main/java/io/flutter/plugins/camera/features/resolution/ResolutionFeature.java#L280-L291

Maybe it will help someone to figure out what is going on.

The simplest workaround for now is setting these values in your `build.gradle`:

```
        compileSdkVersion = 33
        targetSdkVersion = 32
```

Then you'll need to handle the notification permissions behaviour changes:

https://notificare.com/blog/2022/09/16/deep-dive-into-android-notifications-permission/
/cc @reidbaker to route for investigation.

Thank you @acoutts for the in depth explanation! The [API](https://developer.android.com/reference/android/media/CamcorderProfile#getAll(java.lang.String,%20int)) that you listed is the cause of the issue. It seems to be returning a list of size 1 with entry `null`.

This is a known Android issue, and should be fixed as of the 2022-11-05 build release (those starting with TQ1A*, see [docs](https://source.android.com/docs/setup/about/build-numbers#source-code-tags-and-builds) for more information). **If anyone is still experiencing this issue on any of these builds or a later build, please follow up in this bug.**

The update should fix the bug, but I will consider creating a PR to temporarily fall through to the deprecated APIs if the newer one fails as this bug explains. I will update here with progress.
